sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The SPACE only changed the HUD (nodes 10 and 15 by 1 pixel each). The gameplay objects didn't change. So SPACE only works when there's something to pour - the first SPACE worked because the white block was being filled. Now the bottom block has two separate parts (node 13 = purple, node 14 = white), and SPACE can't pour into them anymore because they're separate objects.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics:
- Current main area: top purple block (node 12, 84 pixels), middle purple strip (node 13, 50 pixels), bottom white strip (node 14, 50 pixels)
- Goal (top-left): white block on top (50 pixels), purple block on bottom (50 pixels)
The bottom area has two 50-pixel blocks: purple on top, white on bottom. The goal wants white on top, purple on bottom. So I need to swap these two blocks' colors.
Maybe I need to click directly on the white block (node 14) to select it, then click a color indicator to change its color. Or maybe I need to click on the blocks to select them, then use SPACE or color indicators.
Let me try clicking on the white portion of the bottom block (node 14) to select it, then click the purple indicator to change it to purple.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# SPACE only changed HUD. The bottom block has two parts: purple (node 13) and white (node 14).
# Goal: white on top, purple on bottom in the main area.
# Need to swap colors of the two bottom blocks.
# Let me try clicking on the white block (node 14, rows 39-43) to select it, then change its color.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 41, 'col': 31}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
